pattern: A single left-to-right scan with a tag stack, treating CDATA spans as opaque text, validates well-formed nesting where the whole snippet must sit inside one outer tag.
hints: Walk the string once, recognizing three token kinds at a '<': an opening tag, a closing tag, and a CDATA block.
Push opening tag names on a stack and pop-match on closing tags; CDATA content is skipped verbatim and is only legal while inside a tag.

Require the snippet to open with a valid tag (1-9 uppercase letters). Maintain a stack: on '</NAME>' the top must equal NAME. Plain '<' or text outside any open tag is invalid; the stack must be empty exactly when the scan ends at the last character.

edge_inputs.description: valid tag containing cdata with tag-like text
tricky cdata and stray brackets that stay valid
content not wrapped in any tag
tag name too long is invalid
characters after the closing outer tag

stress.description: randomly assembled snippets from valid and corrupting fragments
stress.note: mixes well-formed nesting, cdata, and random corruptions
